R : Type ?u.16996

    have h₂ : a + b ∈ J := by

      apply Ideal.add_mem
      · exact ha.2
      · exact hb.2
    
    -- Since a + b is in both I and J, it is in the intersection I ∩ J.
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

intro a b ha hb
    -- Since I and J are ideals, they are closed under addition.
    -- We need to show that a + b is in both I and J.
    have h₁ : a + b ∈ I := by
      -- a is in I (from ha : a ∈ I ∩ J)
      -- b is in I (from hb : b ∈ I ∩ J)
      -- Since I is closed under addition, a + b is in I.
      have h₂ : a ∈ I := ha.1
      have h₃ : b ∈ I := hb.1
      exact I.add_mem h₂ h₃
    have h₂ : a + b ∈ J := by
      -- a is in J (from ha : a ∈ I ∩ J)
      -- b is in J (from hb : b ∈ I ∩ J)
      -- Since J is closed under addition, a + b is in J.
      have h₃ : a ∈ J := ha.2
      have h₄ : b ∈ J := hb.2
      exact J.add_mem h₃ h₄
    -- Since a + b is in both I and J, it is in I ∩ J.
    exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
